task_description: Critically evaluate the ecological validity of the Discovery method: does performance on 2009-2010 Twitter generalise to modern social media platforms with different character limits, media types, and user behaviours?
adaptation_notes: This is a written critical analysis task with no code. LLM helped structure the argument and identify the three main external validity threats: platform drift, character limit changes (140 to 280), and the shift from text-only to multimedia posts.
top_5_prompts: What are the main threats to external validity when applying the Discovery method (calibrated on 2009 Twitter) to modern social media platforms?
How does the expansion of Twitter's character limit from 140 to 280 characters affect the 100-character proximity threshold?
Does the method generalise to non-English corpora and if not, what components would need to be changed?
What would need to change in the pipeline to handle hashtags, emoji, and image captions that did not exist or were rare in 2009 Twitter?
Compare the Discovery method against modern transformer-based trend detection approaches: where does it excel and where does it fall short?
